channel.title: Comments for page "Joey Fucknuts Builds a Flying Machine"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Joey Fucknuts Builds a Flying Machine"

item.title: Gavin, is that you?
item.content:encoded: Is the part where Joey says the sky is bigger than the ground a reference to Gavin Free(The Dumbest Man in the World) of Rooster Teeth?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I got a PM about this—and I had a weird deja vu moment, because I could've sworn I'd read and upvoted and commented on this in the past, but… apparently that hadn't happened. So I will set out to rectify it.

First off though—I wholeheartedly believe this deserves its rating and could be even higher-rated. It's an excellent read, and definitely has a nice different sort of tone compared to the tales I typically read or review. It's goofy in a charming way, and while there is an article linked I don't feel like I'm required to read the related SCP to understand or enjoy the tale.

As a big fan of lists and tidily arranged information, the numbered format of the tale was appealing. The way Joey perceives everyday concepts is hilarious because of the change in viewpoint. At work, my coworker and I sometimes yell at each other in language that has effectively regressed to Simple Wikipedia mixed with niche internet memes, and "USES BIRD ARMS TO STAY UP???" made me laugh because it reminds me of joking around at work.

The simple but earnest/genuine sentences are hilarious. "Joey did not trust them, and suspected that clouds were things made by wizards, or, more horrifyingly, were wizards themselves." is a great line. There are many great lines in this piece.

Granted, the ending started to lose me a little bit because I was getting a sense of not totally understanding the broader context since I haven't read SCP-3885, but the last part was cute and made me curious about the main article, which is something that all good tales should do.

Overall, a solid read! It's always nice to read tales that give us a different perspective of quirky pieces of the Foundation-verse that the Foundation itself doesn't get to see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I highly recommend imagining all the narration in the voice of John Cleese.

I have painting to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Joey. I don't think you'll be able to hear this, seeing as I am a real human being and you are a fictional construct, but…

If, somehow, by the grace of your author you receive this message, I want you to know that we all believe in you. You can do it, Mayor Fucknuts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This whole story is a drug trip

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
3 - SUN
FUCK FUCK FUCK SHIT OW FUCK

I LOLed.

And Joey Fucknuts flew. Maybe not physically, but certainly in ratings.

Cloud wizards got nothing on Fucknuts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only complaint is that Joey only decides to build a flying machine. Please, please write the story of when he actually does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, I'm convinced.

I want a Vulture Gulch version of the Foundation.

With Joey Fucknuts as the Founder/Administrator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've never wanted an adorable zombie to build an airplane more than I do right now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuckin' amazing. I've never fallen so hard for an article. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Joey liked learning, which is why he was mayor."

Upvoted immediately at that line. This needs to be a series, which then needs to be a canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so weird. Joey's being the smartest of the corpses, along with knowing he is the smartest, but knowing he isn't legitimately smart, but wanting to be really smart and learn things, and also being shy I guess, makes him the most endearing gearhead corpse idiot I have ever read about. This is phenomenal. I want so, so badly for Joey to succeed. I have never felt this connected to zombies. +1 +1 +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WHY IS THIS SO WHOLESOME AND PURE????

Joey Fucknuts for mayor of america 2018.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't think it was possible for me to love those rascals any more than I already did, but you sure proved me wrong. Wonderful work.

item.title: let us take to the skies
item.content:encoded:
godspeed, Joey Fucknuts.

this stunning piece was done by Scorpion451, who is a true rip-roaring thunderface with a heart of molten iron. their artwork thread can be found here. do look - there are many heavy metal pictures there.
